mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer spending and construction activity, generating positive earnings revisions that lift S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 50% of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side slack, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns during late-cycle phases, with Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces anticipatory monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumer purchasing power, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero predictive power for 60-day SPX direction across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in real GDP growth and retail sales indicates a broad demand contraction that suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.5% and RSAFS monthly change < 0%
failure_condition: Combined GDP and retail sales weakness fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with accuracy below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in industrial production signals improving factory utilization and supply-side momentum, triggering short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production spikes are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of observed instances.
